name: Bank Of Dickson
description_short: Bank of Dickson is a local FDIC-insured bank operating since 1954 with four branches in Dickson, Tennessee. Offers personal and business checking, savings, lending, investments, and debit cards.
description_long:
Bank of Dickson has been serving the Dickson, Tennessee community for nearly 70 years since its founding in 1954. The bank operates as a traditional community financial institution with FDIC insurance backing, providing customers with the security of federal deposit protection. The bank maintains four physical branch locations in the Dickson area and is led by President and CEO Jamie Snyder, with a board of local business leaders providing governance and oversight.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services for both personal and business customers. Personal services include checking and savings accounts, investments, debit cards with purchase rewards, and safe deposit boxes. For businesses, they provide commercial checking and savings accounts, business investments, debit cards, and various lending products. Lending services span home loans, personal loans, business loans, and credit cards, with flexible loan payment options available. The bank also provides modern digital banking through eBanking, eStatements, and mobile banking applications for customers who prefer to bank remotely.

Bank of Dickson distinguishes itself as a local community bank with deep roots in Dickson, maintaining four convenient physical branch locations with consistent operating hours. The bank emphasizes personal service with named branch managers and specific contact persons listed for each location. Their debit cards feature purchase rewards programs, and they offer travel notification features through Card Controls to protect customers when traveling. The bank actively promotes security awareness through a Security Center and maintains transparent disclosures for customer protection.

As a community bank, Bank of Dickson serves customers seeking personalized, locally-based banking services with FDIC protection. The main caveat is that as a regional bank with only four branches, customers outside the Dickson, Tennessee area would not have physical branch access and would rely entirely on mobile and online banking options. Their product offerings are standard for community banks but may not offer the extensive range or competitive rates found at larger national institutions or online-only banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit cards with purchase rewards program
Home loans for financing dream homes
Travel notification features through Card Controls
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Four convenient local branch locations in Dickson with consistent lobby and drive-thru hours (M-Th 8am-4pm, F 8am-5pm)
Purchase rewards program available on debit cards to maximize everyday spending
Mobile banking app enables customers to bank from anywhere, anytime
Established community presence since 1954 with stable, local leadership including named branch managers
Travel notification features through Card Controls to protect debit cards while traveling
Comprehensive lending products including home loans, personal loans, and business loans with flexible payment options
Safe deposit boxes available for document and valuables storage
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only four branches, all located in Dickson, Tennessee—no branch access for customers outside the area
As a smaller regional bank, likely cannot offer the competitive rates or extensive product features of larger national banks
No specific information provided about minimum account balances, monthly fees, or interest rates on savings/checking products
Limited online presence with basic website information; no published details about account features, fee schedules, or loan terms
Customer service contact limited to single phone number (615-446-3732) across all branch locations
best_for: Dickson, Tennessee residents and business owners seeking personalized, community-based banking with FDIC protection
Local businesses needing commercial checking, savings, and business lending from a familiar local institution
Customers who value face-to-face banking relationships and prefer dealing with named branch managers
Individuals seeking home purchase financing with a lender that has been established in the community since 1954
diagnosis: Bank of Dickson is best for Dickson-area residents and local businesses seeking traditional, community-based banking with FDIC protection and personalized service from established local branch managers. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside Dickson, Tennessee will have no physical branch access and must rely on mobile/online banking, while pricing and product competitiveness compared to larger national banks are unknown.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Bank of Dickson is a local bank operating since 1954 with four Dickson branches. Offers personal and business checking, savings, lending, and investments.
